person.name: Robert de Clermont de Velay
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Hugues Damas III qui serait celui cité dans la cession faite en 1188-92 , par Humbert III de Beaujeu à Guy II, comte de Forez de l'hommage d'un certain nombre de fiefs dont Néronde, appartenant à cet Hugues Damas, "... feudum Ugonis Dalmacii, silicet Neirundam cum aliis feudis" ; il se qualifiait de vicomte de Chalon et seigneur de Marcilly-en-Chaunois. En 1215, Robert V, comte d'Auvergne réussit à le mettre d'accord avec Agnon IV de Meymont pour régler la succession d'Olliergues. Albert de Cousan et son fils figurent en premier parmi ses témoins dans l'acte concrétisant cet accord: : "Hujus rei testes sunt ex parte Ugonis Dalmacii, Arbertus de Cosant et filius ejus" . C'est cet Hugues Damas III qui aurait épousé la Jeanne Liéras que Marcel Canat prétend avoir apporté Cousan aux Damas . Aussi, nous dit-il se référant à La Mure, "Hugues Dalmace put, le 8 des Ides de mai 1222, recevoir d'Humbert VI de Beaujeu, le château de Cousan en fief de ce prince" . (Ici Marcel Canat a fait une confusion, l'accord de mai 1222 (Forez, t. I, p. 36) concrétise la fin des hostilités entre Guy IV et Humbert V de Beaujeu, dans lequel il est spécifié qu'Humbert remettrait à Guy IV, l'hommage de Cousan)
Sources :
- - FOREZ : GUICHARD (G.), NEUFBOURG (Cte de), PERROY (Ed.), DUFOUR (J.E.). Chartes du Forez antérieures au XIVe siècle, Mâcon, 1933-1980, 24 vol.
- LAINÉ : LAINÉ (M.). Généalogie de la maison de Damas, Paris, Béthune et Plon, 1836, 264 p. in-12.
- LA MURE : LA MURE (Jean-Marie). Histoire des ducs de Bourbon et des comtes de Forez, éd. Chantelauze, Lyon, 1860-1868. 4 vol., in-4°.
relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon Mangas

zh.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay於大約1142年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。他和Iseult de Meymont至少有1個兒子。他去世於1222年在他的家鄉享年81歲，埋葬在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay于大约1142年出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。他和Iseult de Meymont至少有1个儿子。他去世于1222年在他的家乡享年81岁，埋葬在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clermont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Georges, Normand, Pierre, Armand, Edouard, Gaston, Ghislaine, Gilles, Henri, Herve, Jean Francois.

French and English (Middlesex, of Norman and French Huguenot origin): habitational name from any of various places in France called (Le) Clermont, from Old French clair, cler ‘bright, clear’ + mont ‘hill’, i.e. a hill that could be seen a long way off. Compare Clairmont .

English (Middlesex, of Norman and possibly also Huguenot origin): variant of Claremont, from the Old French female personal name Claremonde, supposedly from ancient Germanic Claremunda, a hybrid compound of Latin clarus ‘famous’ + ancient Germanic mund ‘protection’.

variants.name: Clairmont
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adelard, Aime, Antoine, Aurore, Fernand.
French: variant of Clermont and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Clairmont is very rare in France.
In some cases possibly also English: variant of Claremont or Clermont . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Velay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slovak (Veľký) and Czech (Velký): nickname from the adjective Slovak veľký, Czech velký ‘big’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay was born about 1142, in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France. He had at least 1 son with Iseult de Meymont. He died in 1222, in his hometown, at the age of 81, and was buried in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay est né environ 1142, à Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Iseult de Meymont. Il est décédé en 1222, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

de.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay wurde um 1142 in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Iseult de Meymont mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1222 im Alter von 81 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay è nato il circa 1142 nel Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Iseult de Meymont. Morì nel 1222, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolto a Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.

ja.Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Franc: フランスアウバーグンプウイ-デ-ドoメオリールグス
ja.Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-: フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランド
ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velayは約1142年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランドで生まれた。彼はIseult de Meymontとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1222年に故郷で81歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay(은)는 대략 1142년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 클레몽-페랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 Iseult de Meymont(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1222년 그의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 클레몽-페랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay nasceu aproximadamente 1142, em Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Iseult de Meymont. Ele faleceu em 1222, em sua cidade natal, com 81 anos, e foi sepultado em Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay родился около 1142 г. в местности клирмонт-фирранд, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Iseult de Meymont. Он умер в 1222 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности клирмонт-фирранд, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Robert de Clermont de Velay nació aproximadamente 1142, en Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Iseult de Meymont. Murió en 1222, en su pueblo, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.
